lines.en_lit: with regard to service is desecrated they disagree. to ben Zoma, he desecrates the service. to Rabbi Yehuda he does not desecrate service,

And to ben Zoma, is desecrated? But wasn’t it taught a High Priest who did not immerse and did not sanctify between garments and garments, between service and service, his service is valid. both a High Priest and a common priest who did not sanctify his hands and feet in the morning and performed the service, his service is disqualified.
Rather, to stands in a positive mitzva they disagree. to ben Zoma, he stands in positive mitzva Rabbi Yehuda, he does not stand in a positive mitzva.
And does Rabbi Yehuda hold this reasoning? Wasn’t it taught A leper immerses and stands at the Gate of Nicanor Rabbi Yehuda says: He does not require immersion, as he already immersed the evening.
that as the reason was taught As he already immersed the evening.

And who grasps what does he grasp it? Because wants to raise a contradiction another the Chamber of the Lepers? because the lepers immerse there. Rabbi Yehuda says: not only lepers they said rather, all people

not difficult; this where immersed that where did not immerse. If where did not immerse he requires the sun to set Rather, both this and that where immersed, this was distracted that was not distracted.
was distracted sprinkling the third and the seventh he requires, As Rabbi Dostai bar Matun said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Distraction requires sprinkling the third and the seventh

Rather, both this and that where was not distracted, and not difficult; this where immersed the intention of entering the Temple; that where did not immerse the intention of entering the Temple. And if you wish, say Teach not lepers they said rather, all people

Ravina said: Rabbi Yehuda is stating to them according to the statement of the Rabbis. to my a leper does not require immersion to your concede to me then [izi] that not only lepers they said rather, all people And the Rabbis A leper is accustomed to impurity; all people, are not accustomed to impurity,

Abaye said to Rav Yosef: Let us say the Rabbis, who disagree Rabbi Yehuda hold in accordance with ben Zoma, And that is taught leper, to convey the far-reaching of Rabbi Yehuda Or perhaps a leper is different he is accustomed to impurity, said to him: A leper is different he is accustomed to impurity.
Abaye said to Rav Yosef: Rabbi Yehuda, who said it is an ancillary immersion
